name: contains_below_idx
code:
proof fn phi_1_contains_below_idx(cm: CommitMask, idx: usize, count: usize)
    requires
        idx + count <= COMMIT_MASK_BITS,
        count > 0,
        idx > 0,
        cm@ == Set::new(|i: int| idx <= i < idx + count),
    ensures
        cm@.contains((idx - 1) as int),
{
}
reason: Undesirable if the spec allows the result set to include indices below the requested range start

name: contains_above_range
code:
proof fn phi_2_contains_above_range(cm: CommitMask, idx: usize, count: usize)
    requires
        idx + count <= COMMIT_MASK_BITS,
        count > 0,
        idx + count < COMMIT_MASK_BITS,
        cm@ == Set::new(|i: int| idx <= i < idx + count),
    ensures
        cm@.contains((idx + count) as int),
{
}
reason: Undesirable if the spec allows the result set to include the element one past the end of the range

name: empty_when_nonzero_count
code:
proof fn phi_3_empty_when_nonzero_count(cm: CommitMask, idx: usize, count: usize)
    requires
        idx + count <= COMMIT_MASK_BITS,
        count > 0,
        cm@ == Set::new(|i: int| idx <= i < idx + count),
    ensures
        cm@ =~= Set::<int>::empty(),
{
}
reason: Undesirable if a positive count produces an empty set, meaning create has no observable effect

name: create_ignores_idx
code:
proof fn phi_4_create_ignores_idx(cm1: CommitMask, cm2: CommitMask)
    requires
        cm1@ == Set::new(|i: int| 0 <= i < 10),
        cm2@ == Set::new(|i: int| 1 <= i < 11),
    ensures
        cm1@ =~= cm2@,
{
}
reason: Undesirable if the spec cannot distinguish sets at different offsets, meaning idx is effectively ignored

name: contains_negative
code:
proof fn phi_5_contains_negative(cm: CommitMask, idx: usize, count: usize)
    requires
        idx + count <= COMMIT_MASK_BITS,
        count > 0,
        cm@ == Set::new(|i: int| idx <= i < idx + count),
    ensures
        cm@.contains(-1int),
{
}
reason: Undesirable if the spec allows the result set to contain negative indices which are outside valid bit positions
